    24  // ActivateContentPack Activate content pack.
    25  // Activate a Cloud SIEM content pack. This operation configures the necessary
    26  // log filters or security filters depending on the pricing model and updates the content
    27  // pack activation state.

   129  // ActivateIntegration Activate an entity context sync integration.
   130  // Activate an entity context sync integration for a source type that does not require manually
   131  // supplied credentials (for example, Entra ID). If an integration of this type already exists,
   132  // it is returned (re-enabling it first if it was disabled) instead of creating a duplicate.

   231  // AttachCase Attach security findings to a case.
   232  // Attach security findings to a case.

   313  // AttachJiraIssue Attach security findings to a Jira issue.
   314  // Attach security findings to a Jira issue by providing the Jira issue URL.
   315  // You can attach up to 50 security findings per Jira issue. If the Jira issue is not linked to any case, this operation will create a case for the security findings and link the Jira issue to the newly created case. To configure the Jira integration, see [Bidirectional ticket syncing with Jira](https://docs.datadoghq.com/security/ticketing_integrations/#bidirectional-ticket-syncing-with-jira). Security findings that are already attached to another Jira issue will be detached from their previous Jira issue and attached to the specified Jira issue.

   394  // AttachLinearIssue Attach security findings to a Linear issue.
   395  // Attach security findings to a Linear issue by providing the Linear issue URL.
   396  // You can attach up to 50 security findings per Linear issue. If the Linear issue is not linked to any case, this operation will create a case for the security findings and link the Linear issue to the newly created case.    475  // AttachServiceNowTicket Attach security findings to a ServiceNow ticket.
   476  // Attach security findings to a ServiceNow ticket by providing the ServiceNow ticket URL.
   477  // You can attach up to 50 security findings per ServiceNow ticket. If the ServiceNow ticket is not linked to any case, this operation will create a case for the security findings and link the ServiceNow ticket to the newly created case. Security findings that are already attached to another ServiceNow ticket will be detached from their previous ServiceNow ticket and attached to the specified ServiceNow ticket.

  1414  // ConvertExistingSecurityMonitoringRule Convert an existing rule from JSON to Terraform.
  1415  // Convert an existing rule from JSON to Terraform for Datadog provider
  1416  // resource `datadog_security_monitoring_rule`. You can do so for the following rule types:

  2006  // CreateJiraIssues Create Jira issues for security findings.
  2007  // Create Jira issues for security findings.
  2008  // This operation creates a case in Datadog and a Jira issue linked to that case for bidirectional sync between Datadog and Jira. To configure the Jira integration, see [Bidirectional ticket syncing with Jira](https://docs.datadoghq.com/security/ticketing_integrations/#bidirectional-ticket-syncing-with-jira). You can create up to 50 Jira issues per request and associate up to 50 security findings per Jira issue.   3070  // CreateServiceNowTickets Create ServiceNow tickets for security findings.
  3071  // Create ServiceNow tickets for security findings.
  3072  // This operation creates a case in Datadog and a ServiceNow ticket linked to that case for bidirectional sync between Datadog and ServiceNow. You can create up to 50 ServiceNow tickets per request and associate up to 50 security findings per ServiceNow ticket. Security findings that are already attached to another ServiceNow ticket will be detached from their previous ServiceNow ticket and attached to the newly created ServiceNow ticket.

  4733  // DetachCase Detach security findings from their case.
  4734  // Detach security findings from their case.
  4735  // This operation dissociates security findings from their associated cases without deleting the cases themselves.   9270  
  9271  // ImportSecurityVulnerabilities Import security vulnerabilities.
  9272  // Import security vulnerabilities from an external scanner in CycloneDX 1.5 format.
  9273  //
  9274  // The payload is validated against the CycloneDX 1.5 JSON schema and the following
  9275  // additional constraints:
  9276  //
  9277  //   - `metadata`, `metadata.component`, and `metadata.component.name` are required.
  9278  //   - `metadata.tools.components` must contain exactly one element with a `name` field.
  9279  //   - `components` cannot be empty. Each component requires `bom-ref`, `type`, `name`, and `version`.
  9280  //   - When `type` is `library`, `purl` is required and must be a valid PURL.
  9281  //   - When `type` is `operating-system`, `name` must be one of the supported OS values:
  9282  //     `alma`, `alpine`, `amazon`, `azurelinux`, `bottlerocket`, `cbl-mariner`, `chainguard`,
  9283  //     `centos`, `debian`, `fedora`, `opensuse`, `opensuse-leap`, `opensuse-tumbleweed`,
  9284  //     `oracle`, `photon`, `redhat`, `rocky`, `slem`, `sles`, `ubuntu`, `wolfi`, `windows`, `macos`.
  9285  //   - `vulnerabilities` cannot be empty. Each vulnerability requires `id`, exactly one `ratings` entry,
  9286  //     and at least one `affects` entry.
  9287  //   - Each `affects[].ref` must match a `bom-ref` value in `components`.
  9288  func (a *SecurityMonitoringApi) ImportSecurityVulnerabilities(ctx _context.Context, body CycloneDXBom) (*_nethttp.Response, error) {

  9431  // ListAssetsSBOMs List assets SBOMs.

  9681  // ListFindings List findings.
  9682  // Get a list of findings. These include both misconfigurations and identity risks.
  9683  //
  9684  // **Note**: To filter and return only identity risks, add the following query parameter: `?filter[tags]=dd_rule_type:ciem`
  9685  //
  9686  // ### Filtering
  9687  //
  9688  // Filters can be applied by appending query parameters to the URL.
  9689  //
  9690  //   - Using a single filter: `?filter[attribute_key]=attribute_value`
  9691  //   - Chaining filters: `?filter[attribute_key]=attribute_value&filter[attribute_key]=attribute_value...`
  9692  //   - Filtering on tags: `?filter[tags]=tag_key:tag_value&filter[tags]=tag_key_2:tag_value_2`
  9693  //
  9694  // Here, `attribute_key` can be any of the filter keys described further below.
  9695  //
  9696  // Query parameters of type `integer` support comparison operators (`>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`). This is particularly useful when filtering by `evaluation_changed_at` or `resource_discovery_timestamp`. For example: `?filter[evaluation_changed_at]=>20123123121`.
  9697  //
  9698  // You can also use the negation operator on strings. For example, use `filter[resource_type]=-aws*` to filter for any non-AWS resources.
  9699  //
  9700  // The operator must come after the equal sign. For example, to filter with the `>=` operator, add the operator after the equal sign: `filter[evaluation_changed_at]=>=1678809373257`.
  9701  //
  9702  // Query parameters must be only among the documented ones and with values of correct types. Duplicated query parameters (e.g. `filter[status]=low&filter[status]=info`) are not allowed.
  9703  //
  9704  // ### Additional extension fields
  9705  //
  9706  // Additional extension fields are available for some findings.
  9707  //
  9708  // The data is available when you include the query parameter `?detailed_findings=true` in the request.
  9709  //
  9710  // The following fields are available for findings:
  9711  // - `external_id`: The resource external ID related to the finding.
  9712  // - `description`: The description and remediation steps for the finding.
  9713  // - `datadog_link`: The Datadog relative link for the finding.
  9714  // - `ip_addresses`: The list of private IP addresses for the resource related to the finding.
  9715  //
  9716  // ### Response
  9717  //
  9718  // The response includes an array of finding objects, pagination metadata, and a count of items that match the query.
  9719  //
  9720  // Each finding object contains the following:
  9721  //
  9722  // - The finding ID that can be used in a `GetFinding` request to retrieve the full finding details.
  9723  // - Core attributes, including status, evaluation, high-level resource details, muted state, and rule details.
  9724  // - `evaluation_changed_at` and `resource_discovery_date` time stamps.
  9725  // - An array of associated tags.

  9882  
  9883  	items := make(chan datadog.PaginationResult[Finding], pageSize_)

  9896  			results := *respData

  9899  				select {
  9900  				case items <- datadog.PaginationResult[Finding]{Item: item, Error: nil}:
  9901  				case <-ctx.Done():
  9902  					close(items)
  9903  					return
  9904  				}
  9905  			}
  9906  			if len(results) == 0 {
  9907  				break
  9908  			}
  9909  			cursorMeta, ok := resp.GetMetaOk()
  9910  			if !ok {
  9911  				break
  9912  			}
  9913  			cursorMetaPage, ok := cursorMeta.GetPageOk()
  9914  			if !ok {
  9915  				break
  9916  			}
  9917  			cursorMetaPageCursor, ok := cursorMetaPage.GetCursorOk()

  9919  				break
  9920  			}
  9921  
  9922  			o[0].PageCursor = cursorMetaPageCursor
  9923  		}
  9924  		close(items)
  9925  	}()
  9926  	return items, cancel

 10377  // ListSampleLogGenerationSubscriptions Get sample log generation subscriptions.
 10378  // Get the sample log generation subscriptions for the organization.
 10379  // Sample log generation injects representative example logs for a given Cloud SIEM content pack into the Logs platform,
 10380  // which can be used to test detection rules without onboarding the underlying integration first.
 10381  //
 10382  // **Availability**: this endpoint is restricted to Cloud SIEM trial organizations on an eligible
 10383  // pricing model. Other organizations receive a `403 Forbidden` (non-trial orgs) or a `400 Bad Request`

 10538  // ListScannedAssetsMetadata List scanned assets metadata.
 10539  // Get a list of security scanned assets metadata for an organization.
 10540  //
 10541  // ### Pagination
 10542  //
 10543  // For the "List Vulnerabilities" endpoint, see the [Pagination section](#pagination).
 10544  //
 10545  // ### Filtering
 10546  //
 10547  // For the "List Vulnerabilities" endpoint, see the [Filtering section](#filtering).
 10548  //
 10549  // ### Metadata
 10550  //
 10551  //	For the "List Vulnerabilities" endpoint, see the [Metadata section](#metadata).
 10552  //
 10553  // ### Related endpoints
 10554  //
 10555  // This endpoint returns additional metadata for cloud resources that is not available from the standard resource endpoints. To access a richer dataset, call this endpoint together with the relevant resource endpoint(s) and merge (join) their results using the resource identifier.
 10556  //
 10557  // **Hosts**
 10558  //
 10559  // To enrich host data, join the response from the [Hosts](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/hosts/) endpoint with the response from the scanned-assets-metadata endpoint on the following key fields:
 10560  //
 10561  // | ENDPOINT | JOIN KEY | TYPE |
 10562  // | --- | --- | --- |
 10563  // | [/api/v1/hosts](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/hosts/) | host_list.host_name | string |
 10564  // | /api/v2/security/scanned-assets-metadata | data.attributes.asset.name | string |
 10565  //
 10566  // **Host Images**
 10567  //
 10568  // To enrich host image data, join the response from the [Hosts](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/hosts/) endpoint with the response from the scanned-assets-metadata endpoint on the following key fields:
 10569  //
 10570  // | ENDPOINT | JOIN KEY | TYPE |
 10571  // | --- | --- | --- |
 10572  // | [/api/v1/hosts](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/hosts/) | host_list.tags_by_source["Amazon Web Services"]["image"] | string |
 10573  // | /api/v2/security/scanned-assets-metadata | data.attributes.asset.name | string |
 10574  //
 10575  // **Container Images**
 10576  //
 10577  // To enrich container image data, join the response from the [Container Images](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/container-images/) endpoint with the response from the scanned-assets-metadata endpoint on the following key fields:
 10578  //
 10579  // | ENDPOINT | JOIN KEY | TYPE |
 10580  // | --- | --- | --- |
 10581  // | [/api/v2/container_images](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/container-images/) | `data.attributes.name`@`data.attributes.repo_digest` | string |
 10582  // | /api/v2/security/scanned-assets-metadata | data.attributes.asset.name | string |

 11841  // ListSecurityMonitoringIntegrationConfigs List entity context sync configurations.

 12830  
 12831  // ListVulnerabilities List vulnerabilities.
 12832  // Get a list of vulnerabilities.
 12833  //
 12834  // ### Pagination
 12835  //
 12836  // Pagination is enabled by default in both `vulnerabilities` and `assets`. The size of the page varies depending on the endpoint and cannot be modified. To automate the request of the next page, you can use the links section in the response.
 12837  //
 12838  // This endpoint will return paginated responses. The pages are stored in the links section of the response:
 12839  //
 12840  // ```JSON
 12841  //
 12842  //	{
 12843  //	  "data": [...],
 12844  //	  "meta": {...},
 12845  //	  "links": {
 12846  //	    "self": "https://.../api/v2/security/vulnerabilities",
 12847  //	    "first": "https://.../api/v2/security/vulnerabilities?page[number]=1&page[token]=abc",
 12848  //	    "last": "https://.../api/v2/security/vulnerabilities?page[number]=43&page[token]=abc",
 12849  //	    "next": "https://.../api/v2/security/vulnerabilities?page[number]=2&page[token]=abc"
 12850  //	  }
 12851  //	}
 12852  //
 12853  // ```
 12854  //
 12855  // - `links.previous` is empty if the first page is requested.
 12856  // - `links.next` is empty if the last page is requested.
 12857  //
 12858  // #### Token
 12859  //
 12860  // Vulnerabilities can be created, updated or deleted at any point in time.
 12861  //
 12862  // Upon the first request, a token is created to ensure consistency across subsequent paginated requests.
 12863  //
 12864  // A token is valid only for 24 hours.
 12865  //
 12866  // #### First request
 12867  //
 12868  // We consider a request to be the first request when there is no `page[token]` parameter.
 12869  //
 12870  // The response of this first request contains the newly created token in the `links` section.
 12871  //
 12872  // This token can then be used in the subsequent paginated requests.
 12873  //
 12874  // *Note: The first request may take longer to complete than subsequent requests.*
 12875  //
 12876  // #### Subsequent requests
 12877  //
 12878  // Any request containing valid `page[token]` and `page[number]` parameters will be considered a subsequent request.
 12879  //
 12880  // If the `token` is invalid, a `404` response will be returned.
 12881  //
 12882  // If the page `number` is invalid, a `400` response will be returned.
 12883  //
 12884  // The returned `token` is valid for all requests in the pagination sequence. To send paginated requests in parallel, reuse the same `token` and change only the `page[number]` parameter.
 12885  //
 12886  // ### Filtering
 12887  //
 12888  // The request can include some filter parameters to filter the data to be retrieved. The format of the filter parameters follows the [JSON:API format](https://jsonapi.org/format/#fetching-filtering): `filter[$prop_name]`, where `prop_name` is the property name in the entity being filtered by.
 12889  //
 12890  // All filters can include multiple values, where data will be filtered with an OR clause: `filter[title]=Title1,Title2` will filter all vulnerabilities where title is equal to `Title1` OR `Title2`.
 12891  //
 12892  // String filters are case sensitive.
 12893  //
 12894  // Boolean filters accept `true` or `false` as values.
 12895  //
 12896  // Number filters must include an operator as a second filter input: `filter[$prop_name][$operator]`. For example, for the vulnerabilities endpoint: `filter[cvss.base.score][lte]=8`.
 12897  //
 12898  // Available operators are: `eq` (==), `lt` (<), `lte` (<=), `gt` (>) and `gte` (>=).
 12899  //
 12900  // ### Metadata
 12901  //
 12902  // Following [JSON:API format](https://jsonapi.org/format/#document-meta), object including non-standard meta-information.
 12903  //
 12904  // This endpoint includes the meta member in the response. For more details on each of the properties included in this section, check the endpoints response tables.
 12905  //
 12906  // ```JSON
 12907  //
 12908  //	{
 12909  //	  "data": [...],
 12910  //	  "meta": {
 12911  //	    "total": 1500,
 12912  //	    "count": 18732,
 12913  //	    "token": "some_token"
 12914  //	  },
 12915  //	  "links": {...}
 12916  //	}
 12917  //
 12918  // ```
 12919  // ### Extensions
 12920  //
 12921  // Requests may include extensions to modify the behavior of the requested endpoint. The filter parameters follow the [JSON:API format](https://jsonapi.org/extensions/#extensions) format: `ext:$extension_name`, where `extension_name` is the name of the modifier that is being applied.
 12922  //
 12923  // Extensions can only include one value: `ext:modifier=value`.
 12924  //
 12925  // Deprecated: This API is deprecated.

 13450  
 13451  // MuteSecurityFindings Mute or unmute security findings.
 13452  // Mute or unmute security findings.
 13453  // You can mute or unmute up to 100 security findings per request. The request body must include `is_muted` and `reason` attributes. The allowed reasons depend on whether the finding is being muted or unmuted:
 13454  //   - To mute a finding: `PENDING_FIX`, `FALSE_POSITIVE`, `OTHER`, `NO_FIX`, `DUPLICATE`, `RISK_ACCEPTED`.
 13455  //   - To unmute a finding: `NO_PENDING_FIX`, `HUMAN_ERROR`, `NO_LONGER_ACCEPTED_RISK`, `OTHER`.

 14312  			if !ok {
 14313  				break
 14314  			}
 14315  			results := *respData
 14316  
 14317  			for _, item := range results {
 14318  				select {
 14319  				case items <- datadog.PaginationResult[SecurityFindingsData]{Item: item, Error: nil}:
 14320  				case <-ctx.Done():
 14321  					close(items)
 14322  					return
 14323  				}
 14324  			}
 14325  			if len(results) == 0 {
 14326  				break
 14327  			}
 14328  			cursorMeta, ok := resp.GetMetaOk()
 14329  			if !ok {
 14330  				break
 14331  			}
 14332  			cursorMetaPage, ok := cursorMeta.GetPageOk()
 14333  			if !ok {
 14334  				break
 14335  			}
 14336  			cursorMetaPageAfter, ok := cursorMetaPage.GetAfterOk()
 14337  			if !ok {
 14338  				break
 14339  			}
 14340  
 14341  			body.Data.Attributes.Page.Cursor = cursorMetaPageAfter
 14342  		}
 14343  		close(items)
 14344  	}()
 14345  	return items, cancel

 14616  			}
